title: Day 7: Container Rules
prompt:
Each non-empty line is a rule describing what one container type holds. A container type is named by exactly two lowercase words (for example, `dim red`). A rule has the form:

`NAME containers hold CONTENTS.`

`CONTENTS` is either the literal words `no other containers`, or a comma-separated list of entries like `2 dim red containers` — a count followed by a two-word container name. The word is `container` when the count is 1 and `containers` otherwise.

A container type can hold a `bright gold` container if it holds one directly, or if it holds some container type that can (recursively) hold a `bright gold` container. Return how many distinct container types can hold a `bright gold` container. `bright gold` itself does not count.

tests.name: two ancestors
args:
dim red containers hold 1 bright gold container.
plain blue containers hold 1 dim red container.
bright gold containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: no gold
args:
plain blue containers hold 1 dim red container.
dim red containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: diamond inheritance
args:
outer one containers hold 1 mid a container, 1 mid b container.
mid a containers hold 1 bright gold container.
mid b containers hold 1 bright gold container.
bright gold containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: deep chain
args:
a one containers hold 1 b two container.
b two containers hold 1 c three container.
c three containers hold 1 bright gold container.
bright gold containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: branching with extras
args:
alpha box containers hold 2 bright gold containers, 1 ignored leaf container.
beta box containers hold 1 alpha box container.
ignored leaf containers hold no other containers.
bright gold containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: gold contains things does not count itself
args:
bright gold containers hold 2 plain red containers.
plain red containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: irrelevant island ignored
args:
lone alpha containers hold 1 lone beta container.
lone beta containers hold no other containers.
shiny silver containers hold 1 bright gold container.
bright gold containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: multiple direct parents
args:
red one containers hold 1 bright gold container.
blue two containers hold 1 bright gold container.
green three containers hold 1 bright gold container.
bright gold containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: cycle-free DAG with shared ancestor
args:
top root containers hold 1 mid one container, 1 mid two container.
mid one containers hold 1 bright gold container.
mid two containers hold 1 mid one container.
bright gold containers hold no other containers.

parts.title: Part 2: Total contents
parts.prompt: Same input. Now compute the total number of containers that end up inside a single `bright gold` container, counted with multiplicities. If a `bright gold` contains 2 `dim red` and each `dim red` contains 3 `plain blue`, the total is `2 + 2 * 3 = 8`. The `bright gold` container itself is not counted. Return that integer.

tests.name: simple chain
args:
bright gold containers hold 2 dim red containers.
dim red containers hold 3 plain blue containers.
plain blue containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: gold has no children
args: bright gold containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: two child colors
args:
bright gold containers hold 1 dim red container, 2 plain blue containers.
dim red containers hold no other containers.
plain blue containers hold 1 tiny pink container.
tiny pink containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: shared subtree
args:
bright gold containers hold 2 stage a containers.
stage a containers hold 2 stage b containers.
stage b containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: deep chain
args:
bright gold containers hold 1 first chamber container.
first chamber containers hold 1 second chamber container.
second chamber containers hold 1 third chamber container.
third chamber containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: single child count multiplies down
args:
bright gold containers hold 3 sub one containers.
sub one containers hold 4 sub two containers.
sub two containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: branching count multiplies cleanly
args:
bright gold containers hold 2 alpha box containers, 3 beta box containers.
alpha box containers hold 1 inner one container.
beta box containers hold no other containers.
inner one containers hold no other containers.

tests.name: diamond pays both paths
args:
bright gold containers hold 2 mid one containers, 3 mid two containers.
mid one containers hold 1 leaf alpha container.
mid two containers hold 1 leaf alpha container.
leaf alpha containers hold no other containers.
